items.hook: Happy to help you lock this in.
items.bridge: For a Chinese restaurant booking, the branch, arrival time, and party size matter more than a quick yes or no.
items.next_step: Ask for branch, time, and number of guests.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route to Catcher if the user replies with reservation facts.

items.hook: That is already enough to start narrowing it down.
items.bridge: Once the time and group size are clear, the next question is usually seating type and any food preferences.
items.next_step: Ask whether they need a private room and any taste restrictions.

items.hook: Private room planning makes sense if the meal matters.
items.bridge: The right fit usually depends on date, party size, budget, and whether the group is family, business, or celebration.
items.next_step: Ask for dining date, guest count, and event type.

items.hook: Choosing for a mixed family table is worth doing a little carefully.
items.bridge: With Chinese food, elders, children, and spice tolerance can change the whole ordering direction.
items.next_step: Ask about party mix and flavor preference.

items.hook: Company meals usually need both atmosphere and logistics to line up.
items.bridge: Guest count, invoice needs, and whether you want private seating all shape the right arrangement.
items.next_step: Ask for date, headcount, private room need, and invoice requirement.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route as a high-value banquet lead.

items.hook: I get why you do not want to gamble on a long wait.
items.bridge: Queue time can change a lot by branch, arrival time, and how many people are coming.
items.next_step: Ask for branch, time, and party size, then guide toward reservation or safer timing.
items.handoff_suggestion: Send to human or booking flow quickly.

items.hook: Happy to point you in a practical direction instead of letting you guess.
items.bridge: The best picks usually depend on whether you want classic flavors, spicy dishes, or something more home-style.
items.next_step: Ask about flavor style and how many people are sharing.
items.handoff_suggestion: Good engagement signal if the user answers with preferences.

items.hook: A tighter budget can still work if we cut the right things.
items.bridge: For group dining, table size, dish count, and whether this is hosting or everyday eating all change the range.
items.next_step: Ask for headcount and whether fullness or hosting impression matters more.
items.handoff_suggestion: Workable lead if the user engages on trade-offs.

items.hook: That is exactly the kind of thing worth checking carefully before you order.
items.bridge: Ingredient details and kitchen handling can vary by dish and branch, so it is better to confirm the exact restriction than guess.
items.next_step: Ask for the restriction, dish, and branch.

items.hook: I am glad you asked before ordering.
items.bridge: Allergy questions should be treated as safety issues, not casual taste preferences.
items.next_step: Ask for the exact allergen and branch, then escalate for confirmation.

items.bridge: With Chinese food, people usually care about different things such as wok aroma, handmade texture, soup depth, or regional style.
items.next_step: Invite the user to name the exact flavor point they doubt.

items.hook: Makes sense to check the offer before you make the trip.
items.bridge: Coupon and group-buy rules often change by branch, meal period, and date.
items.next_step: Ask for branch and dining time.
items.handoff_suggestion: Useful bridge into booking if the user confirms details.

items.hook: I can understand why that would feel frustrating.
items.bridge: The fastest way to check a delivery issue is usually the order time, branch, and a clear photo if there is one.
items.next_step: Move the user to DM for order details and photos.

items.hook: That kind of extra touch can be worth planning ahead.
items.bridge: Custom dishes or birthday arrangements usually depend on branch capability and notice time.
items.next_step: Ask for date, branch, and the exact arrangement they want.

items.hook: Good call to check holiday timing first.
items.bridge: Holiday opening and queue pressure can differ a lot by branch and meal period.
items.next_step: Ask which branch and what time they plan to arrive.
items.handoff_suggestion: Booking-ready if the user has a fixed time.

items.hook: Happy to move faster on this.
items.bridge: The safest first step is still platform DM so reservation or order details do not get scattered in public.
items.next_step: Ask for branch plus booking or order details in DM.
